genre_refs: John Trumbull painting 'The Declaration of Independence'
description: A designed slogan graphic that places a large white serif headline over John Trumbull's famous painting 'The Declaration of Independence' (the drafting committee presenting the document to the Continental Congress in a paneled hall, with the captured British flags on the back wall and delegates in 18th-century dress). A dark gradient is added along the bottom of the painting so the all-caps white text 'AMERICANISM WILL PREVAIL' reads clearly. A small white Department of Labor eagle/seal is centered just above the slogan. The technique fuses founding-era national mythology with a present-day nationalist slogan, lending historical gravitas to an 'America First' message (the tweet pairs it with 'The future belongs to the Patriot, not the globalist. AMERICA FIRST. AMERICANS FIRST.'). Target audience: a patriotic, nationalist public and the administration's base.
